title: salvia phlomoides var. boissieri
intro: Salvia phlomoides var. boissieri, also known as Boissier's sage, is a plant species belonging to the Salvia genus, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and gray-green foliage. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which contribute to its various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cognitive function. Traditionally, Boissier's sage has been used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer relief from anxiety and insomnia. Additionally, the plant's extracts are often used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
